original: Video game Tetris is first released in the Soviet Union by Alexey Pajitnov

A near-Earth asteroid estimated at 10 meters in diameter explodes over the Mediterranean Sea between Greece and Libya, with the resulting explosion estimated to have a force of 26 kilotons, more powerful than the Nagasaki atomic bomb
3 giant turtles found in Bronx sewage plant
Gemini 9 completes 45 orbits after rendezvous with "angry alligator"
Frank Samuelsen and George Harbo leave NY harbor to row across Atlantic; their 55 day record for rowing was not broken for 114 years
The eruption of Novarupta in Alaska begins, the largest volcanic eruption of the 20th century
Jurgen Schull sets world discus record (74.07 m)
The Ashmolean Museum in Oxford, England, opens as the world's first university museum
First air flight out of sight of land (Scotland to Norway)
Soyuz 11 takes 3 cosmonauts to Salyut 1 space station
Alexis St. Martin shot in the stomach and treated by physicianWilliam Beaumonton Mackinac Island. Leads Beaumont to conduct digestion experiments through hole in St. Martin's stomach.
Sam Simon, American television writer and producer, co-developer of The Simpsons, born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2015)
Maya Yang Lin wins competition to design the Vietnam War Memorial
The Beatles meet their producerGeorge Martinfor the first time, and record "Besame Mucho" withPete Beston drums
Ozzie Virgil is 1st Black player for MLB Detroit Tigers
French Open Women's Tennis: 17-year-old GermanSteffi Grafbeats World #1Martina Navratilova6-4, 4-6, 8-6; Graf's first Grand Slam victory
West Indian cricket batsmanBrian Larahits world 1st-class record 501 not out and 390 runs in 1 day for Warwickshire vs Durham at Edgbaston; only quintuple-hundred in first-class history

The Beatles, with Jimmy Nichol substituting for Ringo, arrive in Netherlands, take a whirlwind boat tour through the canals of Amsterdam, and perform two shows at Veilinghal Op Hoop Van Zegen, an auction hall in the village of Blokker
David R Scott, American USAF Colonel and astronaut (Gem 8, Apol 9, 15), born in San Antonio, Texas
Cynthia Rylant, American Newberry Medal winning children's literature author (Missing May), and librarian, born in Hopewell, West Virginia
Karl Ferdinand Braun, German inventor, co-developed wireless telegraphy (Nobel 1909), born in Fulda, Electorate of Hessen, Germany (d. 1918)
Haitian explorer Jean Baptiste-Pointe Dusable settles in Chicago
Richard Hollingshead opens the world's 1st Drive-In movie theater on Admiral Wilson Boulevard in Pennsauken, New Jersey
10" of snowfall in New England, part of a "year without a summer" which followed the eruption of Mount Tambora in Indonesia
Detroit Stars' Bill Gatewood pitches the 1st no-hitter in Negro League history, defeating the Cuban Stars 4-0
Young Men's Christian Association (YMCA) formed byGeorge Williamsin London
Walter Chryslerreorganizes the failing Maxwell Motor Company and founds automobile manufacturerChryslerCorporation in Detroit, Michigan
Moses Kiptanui of Kenya runs world 5,000m record 12:55.30 in Rome, Italy
Soyuz T-13 carries 2 cosmonauts to Salyut 7 space station
Japanese forces retreat, ending Battle of Midway
Henry Morgan is 1st to take off shirt on TV
NY Mets first basemanEddie Murrayrecords his 1,510th run batted in during 15-1 thrashing of Pittsburgh Pirates to pass Mickey Mantle as all-time RBI leader among MLB switch-hitters
Sydney Swans Australian Rules Football forward Tony Lockett breaks Gordon Coventry's VFL/AFL career goals record with 1,300th major early in 145-94 win v Collingwood at the SCG
Stanley Cup Final, Honda Center, Anaheim, CA: Anaheim Ducks defeat Ottawa Senators, 6-2 for a 4-1 series win; Ducks' first Championship
John LennonandYoko Onoappear for an unannounced performance, joiningFrank Zappaon stage at Fillmore East in NYC
The Rolling Stonesrelease music single "(I Can't Get No) Satisfaction"
World War II: All Major League Baseball games are cancelled in honor of the D-Day landings in northern France
MLB Baltimore Orioles manager Jimmy Dykes is ejected from  both games of a doubleheader at Yankee Stadium, NYC
Jay C Buckey, American physician/astronaut (STS 58 alt, sk: 90), born in New York City
David Schultz, American freestyle wrestler (Olympic gold 74kg 1984; World C'ship gold 1983), born in Palo Alto, California (d. 1996)
Vladimír Růžička, Czech ice hockey forward (Olympic gold 1998; World C'ship gold 1984) and coach (World C'ship gold 2005), born in Most, Czech Republic
Ada Kok, Dutch swimmer (Olympic gold 200m butterfly 1968; silver 100m butterfly, 4×100m medley 1964), born in Amsterdam, Netherlands
John Henricks, Australian swimmer (Olympic gold 100m freestyle, 4×200m freestyle 1956), born in Sydney, Australia
Sandra Morgan, Australian swimmer (Olympic gold 4x100m freestyle 1956), born in Tamworth, Australia
Angelo Moriondo, Italian inventor (patented earliest known espresso machine, 1884), born in Turin, Kingdom of Piedmont-Sardinia (now Italy) (d. 1914)
Henry Allingham, oldest male British supercentenarian (113 years, 42 days) and World War I veteran, born in Clapton, London (d. 2009)
Aram Khachaturian, Armenian composer (Gayane; Sabre Dance), born in Tbilisi, Georgia (d. 1978)
Marian Wright-Edelman, American activist and health care president (Children's Defense Fund), born in Bennettsville, South Carolina
Tony Yeboah, Ghanaian soccer striker (59 caps; Eintracht Frankfurt, Leeds United, Hamburger SV), born in Kumasi, Ghana
Phillip Allen Sharp, American geneticist and molecular biologist (Nobel 1993), born in Falmouth, Kentucky
Heinrich Rohrer, Swiss physicist (tunneling microscope-Nobel 1986), born in Buchs, Switzerland (d. 2013)[1]
James Barnor, Ghana's first photojournalist, born in Jamestown, Accra, Gold Coast
Jimmy Jam [James Harris], American songwriter and record producer (Janet Jackson), born in Minneapolis, Minnesota
The Basketball Association of America is formed in New York City
Great Fire in Seattle destroys 25 downtown blocks
British Open Men's Golf, Muirfield: ScotsmanJames Braidwins the first of five Open titles by three strokes overHarry Vardonof Jersey
Bottle with note of June 9, 1910 found in Queensland
MLB New York Giants use plastic batting helmets for 1st time
6th Children's Miracle Network Telethon
Tamil is established as a Classical language by the President of India, Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam in a joint sitting of the two houses of the Indian Parliament.
